purpose: Pure whitelist computation — determines whether a given peer pubkey is an allowed DM sender based on current MLS group membership. Zero IDB, NDK, or React dependencies.

public_api.name: isAllowedDmSender
public_api.signature: isAllowedDmSender(peerHex: string, groups: ReadonlyArray<Group>, ownPubkeyHex: string | null | undefined): boolean
public_api.description: Returns true if and only if peerHex (case-insensitive) appears in group.memberPubkeys of at least one group, peerHex is non-empty, and peerHex does not equal ownPubkeyHex (case-insensitive). Returns false for empty peerHex, self-addressing, and empty groups.

contacts_modification.change: Add optional isAllowed?: (peer: string) => boolean parameter. When provided and returns false for pubkeyHex, the function returns immediately without writing storage. Default behavior (no parameter) is unchanged — all callers that are not on the DM path pass nothing.
contacts_modification.rationale: Optional parameter avoids breaking non-DM callers (contactCache.ts writes group-member profiles; gating that path would incorrectly block profile sync for legitimate contacts). DM-path callers (S2) will pass the whitelist accessor. contactCache.ts and rememberContactsFromGroups do not pass it.
